file_name: VirtuosoSurgicalInc_20191227_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11933379_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_License Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: Prior to first commercial sale of any LICENSED PRODUCT(S) or LICENSED SERVICE(S) as the case may be in any particular country, Company shall establish and maintain, in each country in which Company shall sell LICENSED PRODUCT(S) or LICENSED SERVICE(S), product liability or other appropriate insurance coverage appropriate to the risks involved in marketing LICENSED PRODUCT(S) and/or LICENSED SERVICE(S) and will annually present evidence to JHU that such coverage is being maintained. Upon JHU's request, Company will furnish JHU with a Certificate of Insurance of each product liability insurance policy obtained. JHU shall be listed as an additional insured in Company's said insurance policies. If such Product Liability insurance is underwritten on a 'claims made' basis, Company agrees that any change in underwriters during the term of this Agreement will require the purchase of 'prior acts' coverage to ensure that coverage will be continuous throughout the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Prior to first commercial sale of any LICENSED PRODUCT(S) or LICENSED SERVICE(S) as the case may be in any particular country, Company shall establish and maintain, in each country in which Company shall sell LICENSED PRODUCT(S) or LICENSED SERVICE(S), product liability or other appropriate insurance coverage appropriate to the risks involved in marketing LICENSED PRODUCT(S) and/or LICENSED SERVICE(S). Upon JHU's request, Company will furnish JHU with a Certificate of Insurance of each product liability insurance policy obtained.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement for annual proof of insurance and continuous 'prior acts' coverage if the underwriter changes weakens JHU's protection against product liability claims. This creates potential legal risk.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Maryland's requirement for maintaining adequate insurance coverage and providing proof thereof in commercial contracts.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text removes the stipulation that the company will 'annually present evidence to JHU that such coverage is being maintained' and the 'prior acts' coverage. While Maryland law does not explicitly mandate annual proof in all insurance contexts, this type of proof is common practice for liability policies and this is a commercial contract operating in Maryland. Maryland law requires adequate coverage, but this omission weakens the landlord's ability to ensure compliance with that requirement. Additionally, it can create an illusion that the annual verification is at their own discretion which is misleading to the insured.

(a) (1) In this section the following words have the meanings indicated. (2) “Backplant” means a title plant that antedates the period of time covered by an existing title plant, or that covers additional parcels of land. (3) “Title plant” means an integrated and indexed collection of title records consisting of documents, maps, surveys, or entries affecting title to real property or any interest in or encumbrance on the property which has been filed or recorded in the jurisdiction for which the title plant is established or maintained, as well as copies of prior title insurance policies. (b) A title plant shall be allowed as an admitted asset for a title insurer under § 5-101 of this subtitle subject to the provisions of this section. (c) (1) The costs specified in this subsection shall be capitalized costs with reference to the valuation of a title plant. (2) Until a title plant can be used by an insurer to conduct title searches and issue title insurance policies, the costs incurred to construct a title plant, including the costs incurred to obtain, organize, and summarize historical information in an efficient and useful manner shall be capitalized costs. The capitalized costs must be directly related to, and properly identified with, the activities necessary to construct the title plant. (3) (i) The costs to purchase a title plant, including a purchase of an undivided interest in a title plant, shall be capitalized costs, recorded at cost at the date of acquisition. (ii) For a title plant acquired separately, the costs shall be measured by the fair market value of the consideration given. (iii) For a title plant acquired as part of a group of assets, the costs shall be measured first by the fair market value of the consideration given, and then the cost shall be allocated to the title plant based on the fair market value of the title plant in relation to the total fair market value of the group of assets acquired. perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the obligation for the company to annually provide proof of insurance coverage to JHU and eliminates the requirement for 'prior acts' coverage when changing underwriters. This omission directly weakens JHU's ability to ensure compliance with adequate liability coverage. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 focuses on title insurance, the Law Explanation suggests that annual proof of coverage is a common practice for liability policies. The absence of these requirements in the modified text creates a legal loophole, making it more difficult for JHU to verify that the company maintains adequate and continuous coverage, thus increasing their legal risk. The Perturbation Explanation also highlights that the omission weakens JHU's protection against liability claims and creates potential legal risk. Thus, there is a legal contradiction.
